works.title: Christ ist erstanden
works.wikipedia_extract: Christ ist erstanden, SWV 470, is a sacred choral work by Heinrich Schütz, a setting of the German Easter hymn "Christ ist erstanden". It is scored for a three-part Favoritchor SAT, two Capellchor SATB, a choir of four trombones, a choir of four viols and basso continuo.

works.title: Christmas Story
works.wikipedia_extract: The Christmas Story (Weihnachtshistorie), SWV 435, is a musical setting of the Nativity in German by Heinrich Schütz, probably first performed in 1660 in Dresden. It was published as Historia der Geburt Jesu Christi.

works.title: Dafne
works.wikipedia_extract: Die Dafne (1627) is an opera. Its libretto was written by Martin Opitz and its music was composed by Heinrich Schütz. It has traditionally been regarded as the first German opera, though it has also been proposed more recently that it was in fact a spoken drama with inserted song and ballet numbers.

works.title: Die sieben Worte Jesu Christi am Kreuz
Geistliche Chormusik
works.wikipedia_extract: Geistliche Chormusik is a collection of motets on German texts for choir by the German early Baroque composer and organist Heinrich Schütz. It was printed in Dresden in 1648 as his Opus Undecimum, and comprises 29 individual settings for five to seven voices, which were assigned numbers 369 to 397 in the Schütz-Werke-Verzeichnis (SWV). The original title was Geistliche Chor-Music, Erster Theil which indicates that Schütz planned a second part. It is also known as Geistliche Chor-Music 1648. The collection contains earlier and new works and a German arrangement of a motet by Andrea Gabrieli.

works.title: Jauchzet dem Herren, alle Welt, SWV 36
Magnificat
works.wikipedia_extract: Heinrich Schütz composed four extant settings of the Magnificat or Song of Mary, one of the three New Testament canticles. He set one in Latin and three in German. In the Schütz-Werke-Verzeichnis (SWV), the compositions have the numbers 344, 426, 468 and 494. The settings on the German text are all part of larger groups of works. They are settings of Martin Luther's German Magnificat, "Meine Seele erhebt den Herren". Schütz wrote the compositions for different forces and occasions.

works.title: Wohl denen, die da wandeln
works.wikipedia_extract: Wohl denen, die da wandeln is a hymn with a text by Cornelius Becker, who paraphrased Psalm 119 in 1602. The common melody was composed by Heinrich Schütz in 1661. Versions of it are part of modern Protestant and Catholic hymnals in German.

works.title: list of compositions by Heinrich Schütz
works.wikipedia_extract: There are almost 500 known compositions by Heinrich Schütz. Listed here are most of his compositions in the order of the SWV (Schütz-Werke-Verzeichnis) catalog.
